name: G1→G2 Zone 2 — The High Pass Wandering Monsters
description: Wandering monster table for Zone 2 (Days 3–4): Above the treeline. No shelter. Cold. Giant cairn-road. Traffic thins but what remains is dangerous. Check frequency: once per 8 hours of travel, once per 8 hours of rest.

results.text: 1d3 Frost Giants (patrol from G2, heading back to the Rift) [PF2e Lvl 9 each]
1 Young White Dragon (sub-adult; aggressive; hungry) [PF2e Lvl 8]
1d4 Giant Owls (hunting at dusk/night; not hostile unless threatened) [PF2e Lvl 4 each]
1 Frost Giant Scout (alone; returning to G2 with a report — carries a written message) [PF2e Lvl 9]
2d4 Crag Linnorms (young; nesting in the rocky faces) [PF2e Lvl 5 each]
1d4 Hill Giants (lagging G1 contingent, surprised to see the party) [PF2e Lvl 7 each]
1 Remorhaz, Juvenile (hunting under the snowpack; first sign is snow melting from below) [PF2e Lvl 7]
HAZARD — Mountain Goat Stampede: DC 14 Reflex or knocked prone and 2d6 bludgeoning
3 Frost Giant Sub-Chiefs (off-duty hunting expedition; boisterous, not immediately hostile) [PF2e Lvl 10 each]
1 Adult White Dragon (territorial; claims this pass) [PF2e Lvl 12 — flee or negotiate]
HAZARD — Blizzard: visibility drops to 10 feet; Navigation DC becomes 26; all movement difficult terrain for 1d4 hours
HAZARD — Avalanche: DC 20 Reflex or buried (DC 25 Athletics to escape; 6d6 bludgeoning per round while buried)
